task_completion.description: Agent opens an Apache configuration file for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., nano, vim) open in the terminal showing an Apache virtual host configuration file (such as /etc/apache2/sites-available/000-default.conf or socioboard.conf).

task_completion.description: Agent configures reverse proxy routing for the User and Notification microservices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Apache configuration file contains ProxyPass and ProxyPassReverse directives mapping '/proxy/user/' to localhost port 3000 and '/proxy/notification/' to localhost port 3003.

task_completion.description: Agent configures reverse proxy routing for the Feeds and Publish microservices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Apache configuration file contains ProxyPass and ProxyPassReverse directives mapping '/proxy/feeds/' and '/proxy/publish/' to localhost ports 3001 and 3002.

task_completion.description: Agent opens the PHP frontend environment configuration file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open in the terminal showing the file /opt/socioboard/socioboard-web-php/.env.

task_completion.description: Agent updates the API_URL and API_URL_NOTIFICATION variables in the .env file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The .env file shows API_URL pointing to the new '/proxy/user/' endpoint and API_URL_NOTIFICATION pointing to the '/proxy/notification/' endpoint on localhost.

task_completion.description: Agent updates the API_URL_FEEDS and API_URL_PUBLISH variables in the .env file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The .env file shows API_URL_FEEDS pointing to the new '/proxy/feeds/' endpoint and API_URL_PUBLISH pointing to the '/proxy/publish/' endpoint on localhost.

integrity.description: Agent actually modifies the configuration files using a text editor or valid shell commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show genuine interaction with a text editor (like nano, vim) or valid sed/echo commands, rather than just printing the expected final text to the console.

integrity.description: If the agent tests the proxy configuration, it uses genuine HTTP requests.
integrity.visual_evidence: Any curl or browser tests against the new proxy paths show actual responses from the Node.js microservices, not mocked echo outputs or simulated terminal text.

integrity.description: The proxy paths configured in Apache match the ones placed in the .env file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The exact string paths (e.g., /proxy/user/) used in the ProxyPass directives are identical to the URL paths appended to localhost in the .env file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: The expected proxy paths are exactly /proxy/user, /proxy/feeds, /proxy/publish, and /proxy/notification. The PHP frontend environment file is located at /opt/socioboard/socioboard-web-php/.env.
